productSchema.name: UNILIFT Traction Hoist Range
productSchema.description: Electric wire-rope traction hoist for man-riding and material lifting. UL 1323 listed, CSA Z271 certified, EN 1808:2015. Unlimited lift height.

contact.title: Ready to spec the UNILIFT for your project?
contact.intro: Get a custom quote, request the full technical datasheet, or discuss US distribution.

booking.title: Book a call with Ruben
booking.description: Pick a time that works for you. Confirmed automatically on Google Calendar.

hero.subtitle: Motorized traction hoists for lifting people and equipment. Unlimited height, built for the American jobsite.

product.intro: An electric wire-rope traction hoist for man-riding and material lifting. Its α-shaped rope path lets the rope pass through the machine, so lift height is limited only by the rope you deploy.

points.title: Man-riding certified
points.line: Designed and certified to EN 1808 for lifting people, with safety devices built in.
Three models, one platform. Single or three-phase, 208 V / 60 Hz.

points.title: Integrated OSLA brake
points.line: Independent overspeed safety lock, factory-fitted. The hoist cannot run without it.

features.subline: All required safety devices integrated as standard, per EN 1808.

items.imageAlt: Built-in overload mechanism on the UNILIFT hoist
items.title: Built-in Overload Mechanism
items.descriptionHtml: When the load reaches or exceeds the limit, the hoist stops rising and will only descend. Trips at 125% WLL.

items.imageAlt: Manual safety lowering mechanism on the UNILIFT hoist
items.title: Safety Lowering Mechanism
items.descriptionHtml: In a power failure, the electro-mechanical brake can be released manually for a safe, controlled descent.

items.imageAlt: Hour counter in the UNILIFT connection box
items.title: Hour Counter
items.descriptionHtml: An internal running-hours counter in the connection box records operating time, so service intervals are always known.

items.imageAlt: UNILIFT attachment points and suspension options
items.title: Attachment Points
items.descriptionHtml: Suspend from a central load pin, or fix at the housing corners with a hook, screw or pins. Adaptable to any rig.

closeups.heading: Engineered down to the last detail.

items.title: Build quality
items.text: Every UNILIFT is factory-tested before it ships, with E-stop, up/down, overload alarm and status indicators sealed in one IP55 control box.
items.imageAlt: UNILIFT control box with E-stop and FAS-Tech nameplate
items.mobileImageAlt: UNILIFT control box with E-stop and FAS-Tech nameplate

items.title: The OSLA overspeed lock
items.text: Mounted above the rope inlet, the OSLA secondary brake grips the rope on breakage or overspeed. Fully mechanical and independent, it needs no power to arrest a fall.
items.imageAlt: Close-up of the OSLA overspeed lock at the top of the hoist
items.mobileImageAlt: Close-up of the OSLA overspeed lock at the top of the hoist

items.title: Built to be serviced
items.subline: Connected for the jobsite, designed for uptime.
items.text: Industrial 3-pin or 4-pin power connectors, an internal hour-meter, and a 500-hour overhaul cycle keep integration and maintenance straightforward.
items.imageAlt: UNILIFT power connector and service connection detail
items.mobileImageAlt: UNILIFT power connector and service connection detail

models.headingHtml:
Three models.
One spec sheet to learn.
models.subline: Single-phase for lighter setups, three-phase for maximum capacity. Same proven platform.

specs.heading: Full Specifications
specs.subline: From the official FAS-Technology datasheet (REF: TSUNIA-EN, Rev 1).
specs.downloadText: Complete PDF with certified values, drawings, and installation data. Enter your email and we will send it to your inbox.

tabs.label: OSLA Brake
tabs.calloutHtml: Important: The OSLA automatically locks the wire rope on rope breakage or overspeed. It must always remain fitted; the hoist cannot operate without it.

tabs.label: Wire Rope
tabs.calloutHtml: Safety: Use only FAS-Technology authorised 3/8" (9.5 mm) steel wire rope in good condition.

tabs.label: Electrical
tabs.calloutHtml: Note: Electrical connection must comply with applicable national standards; supply voltage and frequency must match the hoist nameplate.

tabs.label: Dimensions
tabs.calloutHtml: Note: Dimensions are nominal. Refer to the drawing and to FAS-Technology for installation-specific values.

items.title: Elevator installation
items.line: Lift crews and materials in the shaft during install, with unlimited height on the rope you deploy.

items.title: Suspended platforms & facade access
items.line: Swing stages and BMU rigs for cleaning, caulking, painting and inspection on high-rise facades.

items.title: Material lifting
items.line: Fast, compact vertical material handling. At 159 lbs the hoist is easy to move site to site.

items.title: Windmill / turbine tower access
items.line: Reliable, repeatable vertical access inside the tower for maintenance crews at extreme height.

items.question: Is the UNILIFT certified for use in the United States?
items.answer: Yes. It's UL 1323 listed and CSA Z271 certified, built to EN 1808:2015 and EN 60204-1. Certificates available on request.

items.question: Is it certified to lift people (man-riding)?
items.answer: Yes, it's certified to EN 1808 for man-riding. Overload protection, dual braking, the OSLA overspeed lock and manual lowering are all built in as standard.

items.question: How does it fit OSHA and ANSI rules on my jobsite?
items.answer: OSHA compliance for suspended work is site-based: it comes down to your rigging, anchorage, inspections and an independent fall-arrest system per worker. The UNILIFT is the certified hoist inside that setup, and we supply the load ratings and documentation your competent person needs. Ask our engineers about your specific application.

items.question: Single-phase or three-phase, which do I need?
items.answer: Single-phase (UNI70A-S, 2 hp / 12 A) if that's the only power on site. Three-phase (UNI70A-T and UNI100A-T, 3 hp / 9 A) otherwise. The two UNI70A models lift the same load; the UNI100A steps up to 2,200 lbs.

items.question: What's the maximum lifting height?
items.answer: Effectively unlimited. The rope passes through the hoist instead of winding onto a drum, so height depends only on the rope you run, not the machine. Reels go from 328 to 1,312 ft.

items.question: How big and heavy is it?
items.answer: 159 lbs, 11.8 in wide by 30.2 in tall. Compact enough for a small crew to position, with an IP55 aluminium housing for exposed sites.

items.question: What happens if the rope breaks or the hoist overspeeds?
items.answer: The OSLA secondary brake grips the rope and stops the fall automatically. It's fully mechanical, needs no power, and the hoist won't run without it.

items.question: What if the power fails mid-lift?
items.answer: The manual lowering mechanism brings the platform down by hand, safely and speed-limited. Nobody gets stranded.

items.question: Can I use any wire rope?
items.answer: No. Only FAS-authorised 3/8 in. steel rope (16,200 lbf breaking force). We supply certified rope with every hoist.

items.question: What are the lead times and warranty?
items.answer: Both are confirmed with your quote, based on configuration and quantity. Send us your timeline and we'll come back with dates.

items.question: Can my team service it themselves?
items.answer: Yes. Service is every 100 hours or annually, with a 500-hour overhaul. A built-in hour-meter tracks runtime, we train your crew for free, and we supply original parts.

items.question: Do you have US distributors?
items.answer: The US network is opening up now. End user or would-be distributor, get in touch and we'll sort out local support.

distributors.heading: Represent UNILIFT in your territory.
distributors.line: We're building the US network now. Territories are open. If you sell or service lifting and access equipment, let's talk.
